title: Aster DM Healthcare earns Great Place to Work® Certification in all regional markets
summary: The organization recorded an overall Trust Index score of 82%, alongside an employee participation rate of 88%.
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
UAE—Aster DM Healthcare has once again secured the Great Place To Work® certification across the UAE and its key regional markets, including Bahrain, Jordan, Oman, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia.

This recognition underscores the organization’s continued efforts to build a workplace where employees feel valued, supported, and empowered to deliver high-quality patient care.

Notably, this year marks a significant milestone, as all six countries where Aster operates are now certified.

Jordan achieved the recognition for the first time, highlighting the company’s growing commitment to maintaining a consistent and positive employee experience across diverse markets.

As a result, the achievement reflects a unified organizational culture that prioritizes people across its entire regional footprint.

Leadership perspective and employee engagement

Commenting on the achievement, Alisha Moopen, Managing Director and Group CEO, emphasized that employees remain central to Aster’s success.

She noted that the recognition reflects the company’s ongoing efforts to strengthen a culture that supports well-being, professional growth, and long-term career development.

In addition, she highlighted the organization’s commitment to ensuring employees feel inspired and appreciated throughout their journey.

At the same time, Aster reported notable improvements in employee engagement.

The organization recorded an overall Trust Index score of 82%, alongside an employee participation rate of 88%.

These figures indicate strong engagement levels across teams and demonstrate the company’s responsiveness to employee feedback.

Mohammed Alshair, Executive Chairman of Great Place To Work® Middle East, stated that the certification represents a highly regarded achievement that requires consistent focus on employee experience.

He explained that the recognition is based entirely on real-time employee feedback, further emphasizing its credibility.

Moreover, he noted that Aster’s success across all markets reflects its dedication to building an environment where employees feel trusted and empowered.

Workplace initiatives and employee-centric programs

Guided by its core values—passion, compassion, integrity, excellence, unity, and respect—Aster continues to place its workforce at the center of its strategy.

Its brand promise, “We’ll Treat You Well,” extends beyond patient care to include employees, ensuring they receive adequate support and recognition.

Following an employee survey conducted in December 2024, each business unit implemented targeted action plans based on feedback.

Throughout 2025, the company introduced several initiatives, including Frontline Listening Organization (FLO) programs, Leadership Connects sessions, and an Employee Assistance Program (EAP).

Additionally, Aster rolled out well-being training; engagement activities such as sports and recreation programs; internal newsletters; and career development initiatives.

These efforts collectively contributed to improved trust and engagement across functions.

Expanding workforce and healthcare network

Aster employs more than 13,000 people in the UAE and over 15,000 across the region.

In parallel, the company continues to expand its healthcare network, operating 10 hospitals, 114 clinics, and 299 pharmacies in the UAE.

Furthermore, its digital platform, myAster, supports its mission to deliver accessible, affordable, and high-quality healthcare services across communities.
